name: Via Verde - Lumbier, Liedena, Sanguesa/Zangoza - Circular Route
name_original: Vía Verde – Lumbier, Liédena, Sanguesa/Zangoza – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lumbier offers routes through the pre-Pyrenean valleys of Navarre, Spain, characterized by dramatic gorges and verdant river valleys. The region features prominent natural attractions such as the Foz de Lumbier, a spectacular canyon carved by the Irati River, and the larger Foz de Arbayún. Limestone cliffs, diverse forest masses, and historic greenways define the landscape, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Lumbier The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde - Lumbier, Liedena, Sanguesa/Zangoza - Circular Route, a 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route follows sections of the historic Irati Greenway, offering a flat and accessible path. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Foz de Lumbier (Lumbier Gorge) – Lumbier Gorge loop from Lumbier, a moderate 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route leads through the dramatic Foz de Lumbier gorge, showcasing high limestone cliffs and river views. Local touring cyclists also love the Lumbier Gorge – Foz de Lumbier (Lumbier Gorge) loop from Reserva Natural de la Foz de Lumbier, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through the Foz de Lumbier gorge, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Lumbier is defined by dramatic gorges, river valleys, and historic greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Lumbier are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lumbier's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Lumbier?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Lumbier, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy, flat paths to more challenging rides with significant elevation changes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Lumbier?
A: Lumbier offers a good mix of routes for different abilities. You'll find 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 10 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual riders and those seeking a challenge.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Lumbier?
A: The region is renowned for its dramatic natural landscapes. You can cycle through the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier (Lumbier Gorge)&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the Irati River, with its towering limestone cliffs. Keep an eye out for the large colony of Eurasian griffon vultures soaring overhead. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Arbayún&lt;/a&gt; also offers impressive scenery, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5087670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Greenway&lt;/a&gt; provides a unique cycling experience through tunnels.

Q: Are there family-friendly cycling routes in Lumbier?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5087670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, particularly the section through the Foz de Lumbier, is an excellent choice for families. It's a flat, accessible path that follows the old railway track, offering a great reward-effort ratio. Remember to bring a flashlight for the unlit tunnels!

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Lumbier?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969238865/via-verde-lumbier-liedena-sanguesa-zangoza-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde - Lumbier, Liedena, Sanguesa/Zangoza - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 30 km. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier (Lumbier Gorge) – Lumbier Gorge loop from Lumbier&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Lumbier?
A: The routes in Lumbier are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the stunning gorge scenery, the unique experience of riding through old railway tunnels, and the accessibility of the Irati Greenway for all skill levels.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Lumbier?
A: There is a well-organized parking area available at the entrance to the Foz de Lumbier, making it convenient to access the popular cycling routes in the gorge.

Q: How can I get to Lumbier by public transport with my bike?
A: You can typically reach Lumbier by public transport. The ALSA bus service from Pamplona to Lumbier often accommodates bikes, but it's always recommended to check their specific policies and availability for bike transport in advance.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lumbier?
A: Lumbier is enjoyable for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, but the gorges provide some shade. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any unique features or landmarks along the routes besides the gorges?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty of the gorges, you can encounter interesting features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5087670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself is a historic path, following Spain's first electric train line. You'll also pass through unlit tunnels, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel I&lt;/a&gt;, and near the ruins of the historic Devil's Bridge. For a cultural detour, consider a route that passes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/934414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier&lt;/a&gt; or the Romanesque Church of Artaiz.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the cycling routes in Lumbier?
A: The Foz de Lumbier Natural Reserve generally allows dogs, but they must be kept on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially the nesting vultures. Always check specific regulations for any particular route or protected area you plan to visit.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the town of Lumbier offers various amenities, including cafes and pubs where you can refuel after your ride. There are also picnic tables available at the entrance to the Foz de Lumbier for a more casual stop.
tips: An interesting nature reserve where you can see griffon vultures and other bird species. A route that combines sport, nature and history in equal parts.
The FC del Irati Greenway begins its route south of the town of Lumbier (kilometer 0), with the railway line recognizable as another street that reaches the Foz de Lumbier Nature Reserve.

It is said that Sangüesa has a monument on every street. The Camino de Santiago has passed through here for centuries and there are so many things to see. You can see Santa María la Real, the old Romanesque bridge, the San Francisco de Asís convent, the Carmen convent, the Ongay-Vallesantoro palace, etc.
